mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate for equity valuations and lowers corporate borrowing costs, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the high unemployment signal, or out-of-sample accuracy drops below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion, which boosts corporate earnings expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60d returns with a statistically significant coefficient in multivariate regression over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.02 during early-cycle expansions, or 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of rising INDPRO observations.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation rate above 0.4 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: High inflation months do not precede negative 20d returns, with positive returns occurring in > 55% of triggered cases over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, which sustains wage growth and consumer demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal fails to generate positive 60d returns in > 50% of instances, or interaction term loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP confirms macroeconomic expansion, which raises aggregate corporate profits and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive GDP growth quarters are followed by negative 60d returns in > 50% of cases, or the signal Rank IC falls below 0.01 across rolling evaluation windows.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing industrial production combined with a federal funds rate above 2.0 percent indicates a supply-side contraction under restrictive monetary conditions, which suppresses earnings growth and causes negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction signal fails to predict negative 20d returns during late-cycle tightening regimes, with positive returns exceeding 55% of triggered observations.
